mainEntity.name: Is a 'stiff neck' from 20 years of warehouse work actually a work injury?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, this is known as a cumulative trauma injury. If your long-term job duties caused or significantly contributed to your neck condition, you are entitled to workers' compensation benefits in Utah and Idaho.
acceptedAnswer.description:
If your neck injury was caused by a specific trauma (like a fall or car crash), it is a standard injury claim. If it was caused by years of overhead work (like a painter or mechanic), it is categorized as an 'Occupational Disease' or cumulative trauma. The main difference is the 'notice' period and the burden of proof. For occupational neck issues, you often have a longer time to report it (since you didn't know the exact day it started), but you need more aggressive medical testimony to win.

Key factors include whether there was a 'definite time and place' for the injury.

If you are a long-term employee, your neck pain is likely a cumulative issue. Don't let the boss tell you it doesn't count because there wasn't a 'trip and fall.'

Whether it's a sudden accident or a gradual injury, we know how to categorize and prove your claim to the insurance company.
